conbody.p: A package manager is a program that installs, updates, and removes software libraries for you automatically. Instead of downloading files manually, you type a short command and the package manager handles everything.

section.title: Conda: Your Package Manager for This Course
section.p: This course uses Conda, which comes bundled with Miniconda. Conda is popular in the data science and NLP communities because it manages both Python packages and the Python version itself.

section.title: Why You Need the Terminal
section.p: Package managers are controlled by typing commands in the terminal — there is no graphical interface. This is the main reason you need to become comfortable with the terminal. The commands are short and you will only need a handful of them.

example.title: Example: Installing a Package with Conda
example.p: This single command downloads and installs the spaCy NLP library and all its dependencies.
